narrative: The dispensary's POS reconciliation showed a 412-unit METRC variance carried forward from Tuesday, but the discrepancy was never reported to the state and the next-day audit log was simply overwritten. A pallet of vape cartridges left the manufacturing premises without a printed transport manifest and was driven across the Nevada state line to fulfill a wholesale order. Several pre-roll SKUs displayed THC potency of 31% on the label while the underlying lab COA listed 19.8%, and three of those COAs were over a year old, with no current pesticide panel on file. The packaging used a cartoon bear graphic clearly marketed toward minors and lacked child-resistant certification. The owner deducted ordinary advertising, rent, and payroll on the federal return in violation of §280E, and the storefront's $14,000 daily cash deposit at a community bank generated no FinCEN currency transaction report.
